decisions.choice: Moved rationale to top, removed redundant section at end
decisions.reason: Most important 'why' information should come first

decisions.choice: Consolidated coverage formula and gap classification into single section
decisions.reason: Formula appeared in Phase 2 ASSESS and Yield Enrichment sections - single source of truth is clearer
alternatives: Keep formula inline in each phase

decisions.choice: Standardized phase sections to use Process/Exit Criteria format
decisions.reason: Phases 1-3 used 'Actions', phases 4-5 used different terms - standardizing improves scannability

decisions.choice: Tightened yield enrichment and key questions sections
decisions.reason: Removed verbose tables and descriptions without losing essential information

learnings.content: Documentation refactoring follows same discipline as code: establish green baseline with tests, make incremental changes, verify green after each change
Progressive disclosure principle applies to technical docs: rationale and high-level overview before detailed mechanics
Redundancy in docs is easier to spot than in code - formulas and thresholds appearing multiple times is a clear anti-pattern
